title: Implement a Distributed Cache with Invalidation and TTL
statement:
Design a RESTful API with endpoints for storing, retrieving, and invalidating cache entries in a distributed cache system. The cache should have a Time-To-Live (TTL) for each entry, and cache invalidation should be triggered when an entry is updated or deleted in the underlying resource. Your implementation should use Redis as the cache store and handle scenarios like cache expiration, cache invalidation, and cache conflict resolution.

### Endpoints

### Store an entry in the cache

* `POST /cache`:

	* `key`: The cache key
	* `value`: The cache value
	* `ttl`: The time-to-live (in seconds) for the cache entry
	* `resourceId`: The ID of the underlying resource associated with the cache entry

### Retrieve an entry from the cache

* `GET /cache/{key}`:

	* `key`: The cache key

### Invalidate an entry in the cache

* `DELETE /cache/{key}`:

	* `key`: The cache key
	* `resourceId`: The ID of the underlying resource associated with the cache entry

### List all cached entries associated with a resource

* `GET /cache/resource/{resourceId}`:

	* `resourceId`: The ID of the underlying resource
constraints: Must use Redis as the cache store
Cache entries should have a Time-To-Live (TTL)
Cache invalidation should be triggered when an entry is updated or deleted in the underlying resource
Implement cache conflict resolution using a Last-Winner approach
Implement Redis connection pooling to handle high concurrency
Use the Redis `EXPIRE` command to set the TTL for cache entries

* `resourceId`: The ID of the underlying resource associated with the cache entry

Invalidate an entry in the cache

* `key`: The cache key
* `resourceId`: The ID of the underlying resource associated with the cache entry

List all cached entries associated with a resource

* `resourceId`: The ID of the underlying resource
